sources.provider: ButterflyLens derived from the separately identified taxonomy, name, ALA occurrence, and contextual-geography sources
sources.attribution: Australian Biological Resources Study/Australian Faunal Directory; Atlas of Living Australia and contributing resources; GBIF Backbone Taxonomy; iNaturalist Taxonomy; DCCEEW; Source: Australian Bureau of Statistics.
sources.scope_note: Umbrella metadata only. Every named child artifact retains its own source, fingerprint, licence, attribution, sensitive-data state, and downstream release gate.

sources.attribution: Australian Biological Resources Study, Australian Faunal Directory; Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water.
sources.scope_note: Checklist taxonomy only; no source images, logos, Coat of Arms, occurrence records, coordinates, or other third-party media are included.

sources.dataset: ALA Namematching API responses for the frozen AFD scope
sources.attribution: Atlas of Living Australia and Australian Faunal Directory, accessed 17 July 2026.
sources.scope_note: Taxon concepts, identifiers, ranks, classifications, and matcher diagnostics only; AFD provider terms are retained.

sources.dataset: ALA species-index name profiles for exact crosswalk concepts
sources.attribution: Atlas of Living Australia species index and the name authorities identified on each profile, including Australian Faunal Directory.
sources.scope_note: Selected taxon concepts, classifications, scientific synonyms, vernacular-name assertions, and source metadata only. Provider media arrays, occurrence records, coordinates, users, and downloaded images are excluded; each derived assertion retains its source response fingerprint.

sources.attribution: GBIF Backbone Taxonomy, matcher build created 9 July 2026, accessed 17 July 2026.
sources.scope_note: Public taxonomic names, identifiers, classifications, statuses, and matcher diagnostics only; no occurrence or media dataset content.

sources.attribution: iNaturalist Taxonomy, published 1 July 2026, accessed 17 July 2026.
sources.scope_note: The provider EML states that taxonomic information should not represent creative work and may not be subject to intellectual-property restrictions in many jurisdictions. Only factual taxon fields are extracted; no observations, users, vernacular-name files, or media.

sources.attribution: Australian Biological Resources Study/Australian Faunal Directory; Atlas of Living Australia; GBIF Backbone Taxonomy; iNaturalist Taxonomy.
sources.scope_note: Derived factual names and identifiers. Each provider source retains its own rights basis and exact snapshot receipt.

sources.dataset: Empty First Nations language-name contracts and review workflow
sources.attribution: ButterflyLens First Nations Language-Name Governance; AIATSIS, CARE, AustLang, and Local Contexts principles are cited but no community name or knowledge is copied.
sources.scope_note: Original schemas, an empty assertion dataset, an empty append-only decision dataset, and a zero-count review manifest. No First Nations language name, cultural knowledge, community contact, authority decision, or permission evidence is included.

sources.provider: Atlas of Living Australia and identified contributing data resources
sources.dataset: Selected public-compatible Papilionoidea occurrence download for processed country Australia
sources.attribution: Atlas of Living Australia and every contributing data provider and data resource identified in the frozen citation and snapshot receipts.
sources.scope_note: 236,897 public processed occurrence-metadata rows from 53 data resources. Only CC0, Public Domain Mark, and attribution-only CC BY variants are admitted. Per-row licence and provider/resource citation, generalisation, withheld-information, quality, basis, and sensitive-state evidence are retained. No media is included.

sources.dataset: ALA occurrence API OpenAPI and current indexed-field definitions
sources.attribution: Atlas of Living Australia occurrence API documentation and indexed-field metadata.
sources.scope_note: Provider API contracts and factual field metadata only; no credentials, private API data, occurrence media, or sensitive unprocessed values.

sources.provider: Atlas of Living Australia Spatial Service, DCCEEW, and Australian Bureau of Statistics
sources.dataset: ALA spatial API contract and metadata for IBRA v7 regions and Local Government Areas 2023
sources.attribution: Atlas of Living Australia Spatial Service; Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water; Source: Australian Bureau of Statistics.
sources.scope_note: OpenAPI contract and factual layer metadata plus ALA-indexed contextual values. No boundary geometry is copied. LGA values are Mesh Block approximations for statistical use, not official legal boundaries.

sources.provider: ButterflyLens derived from the frozen ALA public occurrence snapshot
sources.attribution: Atlas of Living Australia and every contributing provider/resource in the frozen citation receipt; ButterflyLens normalization schema and provenance.
sources.scope_note: Deterministic typed normalization of all 236,897 selected source rows. Provider taxon, quality, licence, sensitivity, and contextual-geography assertions remain explicit; exact crosswalk matches are not human verification and unmatched values are retained.

sources.provider: ButterflyLens derived from the normalized ALA public occurrence snapshot
sources.dataset: National, contextual, and H3 ALA baseline occurrence-evidence aggregates
sources.attribution: Atlas of Living Australia, every contributing provider/resource, DCCEEW, Source: Australian Bureau of Statistics, and ButterflyLens aggregation provenance.
sources.scope_note: Aggregate counts and lineage digests from 230,027 spatially eligible public rows. Generalised rows are coarse-only; no boundary geometry is copied; H3 centers are not occurrence coordinates; missing evidence does not imply biological absence.

sources.provider: ButterflyLens derived from the frozen ALA occurrence, dataset, and citation receipts
sources.dataset: Published ALA dataset and snapshot evidence manifests
sources.attribution: Atlas of Living Australia and all contributing resources represented in the frozen citation receipt; ButterflyLens publication provenance.
sources.scope_note: Exact UID joins preserve 53 resource citations and 31 non-resource citation entries. Three potentially restrictive citation-rights conflicts covering 16,753 selected rows block downstream public-product release pending resolution or exclusion; the screen is not a legal conclusion.

sources.provider: GBIF and iNaturalist normalized by BioMiner at the pinned origin commit
sources.dataset: Bounded Australian butterfly candidate-observation and media metadata import
sources.attribution: GBIF and its contributing datasets; iNaturalist observations and the creators/rights holders retained per candidate media row; BioMiner normalization provenance.
sources.scope_note: Provider-labelled metadata only: 12,980 observations and 24,329 media candidates from 742 bounded queries. Exact provider identities link 10,453 observation mirror groups and 93 media duplicate hypotheses without asserting byte or perceptual equivalence. This import itself downloaded no image bytes. All media remains unreviewed; its later automated gate is recorded separately; import, taxon, and licence conflicts remain blocked.

sources.provider: iNaturalist open-data media processed by BioMiner at the pinned origin commit
sources.dataset: Automated provisional reference-media gate and local decode evidence
sources.attribution: iNaturalist photo creators and rights holders retained per candidate row; BioMiner download/decode provenance; ButterflyLens gate provenance.
sources.scope_note: A strict CC0/CC-BY, attribution, exact-taxon, mirror-conflict, and approved-host lane selected 2,910 unreviewed provider-labelled rows. Local isolated decoding validated 2,906 objects; four HTTP 404 outcomes remain quarantined. The tracked evidence contains source identities, rights states, checksums, perceptual hashes, dimensions, and command reports but no source-image bytes. GBIF media hosts remain blocked.
